analysis_html:
RIKA Chemical — Discovery / Lunch & Learn (Paylocity + Benefits Overview)

Attendees: Gabe (AE), Josh (Sales Mgr, DFW), Jake (Benefits Advisor), Brandy (HR Manager, RIKA), Brittany (L&D Manager, RIKA), Laisha (HR Associate, RIKA), Miguel (new hire, RIKA), Jerrica (AP Specialist, RIKA). Not present: Rob (VP of HR), Janelle (Controller), Marcia (AR)

Deal Status: This was a first on-site — an educational lunch-and-learn, not a buying conversation. RIKA is a ~100-employee chemical manufacturer currently on Paycom (~2.5 years). Three key decision-makers were absent (VP of HR, Controller, AP lead). Brandy explicitly said 2025 is her "get-my-feet-wet" year and defined strategies come in 2026. She already audited Paycom, found underutilized modules and services they're paying for but not using — that's a real opening, but she's not in buying mode yet. The prospect is warm but early-stage. Biggest risk: no compelling event or timeline. Biggest opportunity: Brandy's dissatisfaction with Paycom utilization, the leadership gap in employee engagement, and RIKA's clear need for IT/HR workflow automation (paper-heavy, fragmented tools, weak offboarding). The benefits conversation (self-funding education via Jake) was pure awareness — no indication they're evaluating brokers. This deal needs significant nurturing before it becomes real pipeline.

Coaching Notes:

  • Three of the most important stakeholders (Rob/VP HR, Janelle/Controller, Marcia/AR) were absent. Gabe acknowledged it but didn't establish a concrete path to get in front of them. The next touch should be specifically designed to engage Rob — he's the decision-maker, and nothing moves without him. Brandy is an influencer and internal champion at best.
  • The meeting ran almost entirely as a Paylocity/benefits education session with minimal true discovery. Gabe uncovered pain signals (underutilized Paycom modules, paper-heavy processes, weak offboarding workflows, no identity management, employee engagement gaps) but didn't quantify any of them or tie them to cost/risk. A follow-up should anchor on building a business case around what RIKA is paying Paycom for vs. what they're actually using — Brandy already did that audit, so asking for it would be high-value.

Next Steps:

  • Gabe — Send follow-up to Brandy with recap and request introduction/meeting with Rob (VP of HR) and Janelle (Controller) — no date stated, needs one
  • Gabe — Ask Brandy for her Paycom audit findings (what they're paying for vs. using) to build a comparison framework — no date — needs one
  • Jake — Follow up with Brandy/Laisha on current benefits funding strategy details (loss ratio, renewal history) to assess self-funding viability — no date — needs one
  • Gabe — Maintain relationship cadence (Brandy indicated openness to "set something up in the future") — schedule next touchpoint before end of Q2 to stay warm through RIKA's year-end evaluation cycle

Market Intel:

  • Paycom (incumbent): ~2.5 years in. Brandy's audit revealed modules being paid for but not utilized. The team acknowledged Paycom doesn't "suffice" for several needs, forcing them to use external tools. No strong loyalty expressed — Brandy's tone was diagnostic, not defensive. Paycom was sourced by a former employee who left the company, meaning no active internal champion for the vendor.
  • Benefits broker (incumbent): ~5 years, sourced by VP of HR from a prior role. Brandy admitted she's still familiarizing herself and acknowledged "there's more we could be doing." Not a ringing endorsement — soft vulnerability.
  • Tech stack gaps: Using Bitwarden (limited rollout, not fully adopted), Microsoft Copilot for AI, no identity management platform (no Okta/OneLogin), no automated offboarding workflow, heavy paper/file cabinet reliance. IT team is 4-5 people with limited bandwidth.

AE Demostack Training — Desktop Setup & Housekeeping

Attendees: JP (Trainer), Lisa (SC Org Leader, Demo Stack), multiple AEs (Randy, Laura, James, Jessica, Meredith, Mike, Rachel, CJ, Paige, others)

Deal Status: N/A — internal enablement session. This was the setup/housekeeping portion of a Demostack training. The transcript cuts off before any substantive demo customization content. Key takeaway: AEs must use the desktop version of Demostack (not browser/My Apps) and close Teams while running it due to Lenovo performance issues. Lisa's team is working on a fix for the Teams conflict. Several reps (Randy, Laura, James, Jessica, Meredith, Mike, Paige) did not have the desktop version installed — worth noting if demo quality issues surface in their pipeline.

Coaching Notes:

  • Multiple reps didn't have Demostack desktop installed, meaning they've been running prospect demos on the browser version (or not at all). If any reps on your team were flagged, it's worth a quick check on whether demo quality has been impacted in active deals.

Next Steps:

  • All AEs — Confirm Demostack desktop version installed, pinned to taskbar, and close Teams before any prospect demo — immediately
  • All AEs — Run Lenovo update (link provided in session) to address machine performance — this week

🔴 Director Priorities — Today

  1. 🔴 Coach Gabe on RIKA next steps — get to Rob (VP of HR). The lunch-and-learn went well relationally, but three decision-makers were absent and no concrete next step was locked. Gabe needs a clear plan to get in front of Rob before this goes cold. Help him craft the ask to Brandy — she's willing but won't drive urgency on her own given her "2025 is observation year" posture.
  2. 🔴 Verify your team's Demostack readiness. Several reps across the org didn't have the desktop version installed. If any are yours, confirm they're set up correctly before their next prospect demo. A glitchy or slow demo in a competitive deal against Paycom is an unforced error.

📊 Pipeline Pulse

Light day for active pipeline movement. The RIKA meeting is a relationship-building early touch, not a deal — it should not be staged as anything beyond initial prospecting until Rob and Janelle are engaged and a timeline surfaces. The Paycom displacement angle is real but latent: Brandy found waste in their Paycom contract, the original champion who sourced Paycom has left the company, and no one on the RIKA team expressed strong loyalty to the platform. The Demostack training is a reminder to pressure-test demo execution hygiene across the team — reps running browser-version demos on underpowered machines is an invisible deal-killer that won't show up in CRM but will show up in win rates.
